title: Hippeastrum pantanalense
intro: Hippeastrum pantanalense, commonly known as the Pantanal amaryllis, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and infections, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its overall health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Hippeastrum pantanalense include alkaloids, such as hippeastrine and galanthamine, as well as other compounds like saponins and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hippeastrum pantanalense include extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Hippeastrum pantanalense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. It belongs to the genus Hippeastrum, which is a group of bulbous plants native to the Americas. Specifically, H. pantanalense is a part of the section Stenopodium, which comprises several species of Hippeastrum.
